metadata.title: SpyCloud Darknet & Identity Threat Exposure Intelligence for Microsoft Sentinel
metadata.description: Complete deployment of SpyCloud Darknet & Identity Threat Exposure Intelligence for Microsoft Sentinel. Includes: CCF data connector (5 REST API pollers), 6 custom tables, 10 remediation playbooks, 49 analytics rules, Fusion multistage ML detection, MSIC rules, UEBA/Anomaly settings, 4 automation rules, 28 hunting queries, 4 watchlists, 3 workbooks, TI enrichment playbook, and Security Copilot integration (85 KQL skills + 12 API skills + interactive agent).
metadata.author: SpyCloud

metadata.description: PRIMARY AZURE REGION — This single selection determines the region for ALL deployed resources: the Log Analytics workspace, Data Collection Endpoint (DCE), Data Collection Rule (DCR), custom tables, and all Sentinel solution artifacts. Defaults to your resource group's region. Only change this if you need resources in a different region than your resource group. IMPORTANT: If you have an existing Log Analytics workspace, select the region where it is already deployed. The Azure Portal will dynamically show all available regions for your subscription — no hardcoded list needed.

OPTIONAL — Override the workspace region if it differs from the primary deployment region above. Leave BLANK to use the primary deployment region for everything (recommended for most deployments). Only set this if your Log Analytics workspace is in a different region than your resource group. When set, all workspace-scoped resources (tables, connectors, DCR, DCE) will deploy to this region instead.

Azure Subscription ID where Microsoft Sentinel is deployed. Auto-detected from your current context. Only override this for cross-subscription deployments where Sentinel is in a different subscription than where you are deploying from.

Resource group containing the Microsoft Sentinel workspace. Auto-detected from your current context. All SpyCloud resources (DCE, DCR, custom tables, connectors) will be deployed into this resource group. Only override if your Sentinel workspace is in a different resource group.

REQUIRED — Name of the Log Analytics workspace for Microsoft Sentinel. If createNewWorkspace is TRUE: enter your desired name (e.g., 'spycloud-supreme-sentinel'). If createNewWorkspace is FALSE: enter the exact name of your existing workspace. To find existing workspaces: Azure Portal → Log Analytics workspaces, or CLI: az monitor log-analytics workspace list --query '[].name' -o tsv

Name for the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) that receives SpyCloud API data. Default: 'dce-spycloud-{workspaceName}' — follows Azure naming best practices. The DCE is the HTTPS ingestion endpoint that the SpyCloud connector sends data to before it reaches your workspace. If a DCE with this name already exists in your resource group, it will be reused. Example: If workspace is 'law-sentinel-prod', DCE will be 'dce-spycloud-law-sentinel-prod'.

Name for the Data Collection Rule (DCR) that transforms and routes SpyCloud data to the correct tables. Default: 'dcr-spycloud-{workspaceName}' — follows Azure naming best practices. The DCR contains KQL transforms that normalize incoming SpyCloud API data and route it to the appropriate custom table (Watchlist, Breach Catalog, MDE Logs, or Conditional Access Logs). Example: If workspace is 'law-sentinel-prod', DCR will be 'dcr-spycloud-law-sentinel-prod'.

Number of days to retain SpyCloud data in Log Analytics custom tables. Applies to ALL four SpyCloud tables. • 30 days = Minimum retention (GDPR-friendly, lowest storage cost) • 60 days = Short-term investigation window • 90 days = RECOMMENDED — supports most SOC investigation workflows and SOC2/NIST baselines • 180 days = Extended — trend analysis, recurring breach pattern detection • 365 days = Full year — required for PCI DSS, annual SOC2 audits, some cyber insurance policies • 730 days = Maximum (2 years) — legal hold, regulatory investigations, litigation support COST IMPACT: Each additional 30 days adds ~1x your monthly ingestion volume to storage costs. For HIPAA (6-year requirement), use 730 days here plus Azure Log Analytics archive/export for long-term.

Azure resource tags applied to ALL deployed resources (DCE, DCR, tables, connectors). Use tags for cost tracking, environment labeling, compliance labeling, and resource governance. Default tags include solution name, vendor, data classification, and management method. Add your organization's required tags (costCenter, owner, project, department) as key-value pairs. Example: Add 'costCenter': 'SEC-OPS-001' and 'owner': 'soc-team@company.com' for chargebacks.

Pricing tier of your Log Analytics workspace (informational — does not change your workspace tier). • PerGB2018 = Pay-As-You-Go (most common, ~$2.76/GB ingested) • CapacityReservation = Committed tier with discounts (100GB/day+, saves 10-25%) • Free = Limited to 500MB/day ingestion (not recommended for SpyCloud) Typical SpyCloud ingestion: 1-50 MB/day (small), 50-500 MB/day (large enterprise). Check Azure Portal → Log Analytics → Usage and estimated costs for your current tier.

Public network access setting for the Data Collection Endpoint (DCE). • 'Enabled' (DEFAULT) = DCE accepts data from the public internet (standard for cloud-to-cloud connectors) • 'Disabled' = DCE only accepts data via Private Link (requires Azure Private Link configuration) For most SpyCloud deployments, 'Enabled' is correct because the Sentinel CCF connector sends data from Azure's internal infrastructure to the DCE. Only set to 'Disabled' if your security policy mandates Private Link for all Monitor ingestion.
Auto-generated deployment timestamp in UTC. Do not modify — ARM populates this automatically at deployment time.
Deploy MDE Device Isolation playbook. REQUIRES: Defender for Endpoint P1/P2. Without license, deploys but cannot function.
Deploy CA Identity Protection playbook. REQUIRES: Entra ID P1/P2. Without license, deploys but cannot reset passwords.
Deploy the SpyCloud Infostealer Detection analytics rule. Creates Sentinel incidents when severity >= 20 exposures are detected.
Deploy the automation rule that connects the analytics rule to the playbooks for automatic remediation on incident creation.

OPTIONAL — Object ID of the Entra ID security group to add compromised users to. This group should be targeted by a Conditional Access policy that restricts access. Create the group first: Entra ID → Groups → New group → Security. Leave blank to skip CA group assignment.
Device isolation type for MDE remediation. 'Full' = complete network isolation (recommended for infostealer response). 'Selective' = allow Outlook, Teams, and Defender communication.
Tag name applied to compromised devices in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint. Used for tracking and filtering in the MDE portal.
Severity level assigned to Sentinel incidents created by the SpyCloud infostealer analytics rule.
How often the analytics rule runs. PT1H = every hour (recommended). PT5M = every 5 minutes (near real-time, higher cost).
Minimum SpyCloud severity to trigger remediation. 20 = infostealer credentials (recommended). 2 = all breaches (high volume). 25 = infostealer + stolen sessions only.
Create an Azure Monitor Action Group for email/webhook notifications when SpyCloud incidents are created or data ingestion stops.
OPTIONAL — Email address to receive alert notifications for SpyCloud incidents and data health issues. Required if enableNotifications is TRUE.
OPTIONAL — Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for alert notifications. Create one in Teams: Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys the full library of 49 SpyCloud analytics rules covering infostealer detection, plaintext password alerts, PII/SSN exposure, session cookie theft, device reinfection, credential reuse, geographic anomalies, VIP exposure, and remediation gap detection. Rules deploy ENABLED by default with recommended settings. Disable individual rules post-deployment in Sentinel > Analytics if needed.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys rules for detecting stolen session cookies and tokens (severity 25). These are the most critical exposures as they enable MFA bypass via session hijacking, requiring immediate session revocation and device isolation.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys rules correlating SpyCloud exposures with third-party identity provider logs (Okta, Duo, Ping Identity, CyberArk). Detects compromised credentials being used across IdP sign-in events. Rules auto-skip gracefully if the required IdP connector is not installed.

Set to TRUE to create a new Log Analytics workspace AND enable Microsoft Sentinel on it. Set to FALSE if you already have a workspace with Sentinel enabled. When TRUE, a new workspace is created using the name in the 'workspace' parameter.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Get after deployment via post-deploy.sh or Azure Portal.
LEAVE BLANK on first deploy. Get after deployment via post-deploy.sh or Azure Portal.

RECOMMENDED: ON — After ARM deployment, run scripts/post-deploy-auto.sh to automatically resolve DCE/DCR, assign RBAC roles, grant managed identity permissions, grant admin consent, enable rules, and verify deployment.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Credential response playbook with password reset + Teams alert notification. Combines identity remediation with SOC awareness. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2 for password reset; Teams notifications work without additional license.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Scheduled MDE blocklist scan that auto-isolates devices matching severity 25 infostealer records. Runs on configurable schedule (default: every 4 hours). Requires: MDE P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

Microsoft Teams incoming webhook URL for SOC alert notifications. Used by the Credential Response playbook to send real-time alerts to your security operations channel. Leave blank to skip Teams integration.
ServiceNow instance URL (e.g., https://your-instance.service-now.com) for automatic ticket creation. Leave blank to skip ServiceNow integration.
How often (in hours) the MDE Blocklist playbook runs to scan for new severity 25 infostealer matches against Defender for Endpoint. Default: every 4 hours.

Deploy the SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Dashboard workbook in Sentinel. Provides real-time charts for exposure overview, severity trends, top users, infected devices, password analysis, remediation status, and breach catalog.

Enable User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) in Microsoft Sentinel. UEBA identifies anomalous behavior by building behavioral profiles of users and entities. Required for SpyCloud analytics rule #29 (UEBA Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User). Enables: Entra ID entity provider, SigninLogs, AuditLogs, AzureActivity data sources.

Enable built-in Anomaly detection rules in Microsoft Sentinel. ML-powered anomaly detection identifies unusual patterns in sign-in behavior, resource access, and network activity. Complements SpyCloud credential exposure alerts with behavioral context.

WARNING: Set to FALSE if Fusion is already enabled in your workspace (default). The Fusion template can only be installed ONCE per workspace. Only set to true on a brand-new Sentinel workspace where Fusion has never been enabled.

Deploy Microsoft Security Incident Creation rules from Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, and MCAS. Set to true only on first deployment - these built-in rules require ETag for updates and cannot be redeployed.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Enriches incidents with VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB, and GreyNoise threat intelligence lookups for IPs found in infostealer data. Free tier API keys supported. If no VirusTotal API key is provided, the playbook deploys but skips TI lookups until configured.
VirusTotal API key for the TI Enrichment playbook. Free tier allows 4 requests/minute. Get yours at: https://www.virustotal.com/gui/my-apikey
Environment tag applied to all resources.
Owner tag — team or individual responsible.
Cost center tag for billing.
Pricing tier for new Log Analytics workspace (only when createNewWorkspace=TRUE). PerGB2018=Pay-As-You-Go, CapacityReservation=committed discounts.

Slack Incoming Webhook URL for SOC notifications. Logic Apps will send alerts here when incidents are created. Leave blank to skip Slack integration. Create at: api.slack.com/apps → Incoming Webhooks.
Jira Automation webhook URL for auto-creating issues on credential exposure. Leave blank to skip.
Azure DevOps Service Hook URL for auto-creating work items. Leave blank to skip.
Deployment mode: Full (first install), Upgrade (add features), or selective component deployment.
Deploy core SpyCloud detection analytics rules sc-001 through sc-012. These are the foundational infostealer and credential exposure detection rules recommended for all deployments.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys O365 & Entra ID correlation rules (sc-020 to sc-029). Correlates SpyCloud exposures with sign-in activity, impossible travel, mailbox rules, OAuth consent, and admin role changes. Requires: Entra ID and Office 365 connectors. Rules auto-skip if data sources are not connected.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys UEBA & firewall correlation rules (sc-030 to sc-039). Detects anomalous behavior, lateral movement, DNS C2 communication, and VPN anomalies from exposed users. Requires: UEBA enabled + firewall/CEF connector. Rules auto-skip if data sources are not connected.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deploys advanced threat detection rules (sc-040 to sc-049). Detects multi-stage attack chains, CA bypass attempts, credential re-exposure after reset, TI indicator matches, and geographic mismatches. Uses multiple data sources with graceful fallback.

Deploy 5 Microsoft Security Incident Creation (MSIC) rules that auto-create Sentinel incidents from Defender XDR, Entra ID Protection, Defender for Cloud, Microsoft Cloud App Security, and Azure Active Directory Identity Protection alerts.

Deploy the Fusion Advanced Multistage Attack Detection rule. Uses ML to correlate low-fidelity alerts across multiple data sources into high-confidence multi-stage attack incidents spanning the kill chain.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Forces password change on next sign-in via Microsoft Graph API when credentials are exposed. The user must re-authenticate with MFA. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2. Post-deploy: run scripts/post-deploy-auto.sh to auto-grant User.ReadWrite.All permission.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Instantly revokes ALL active sessions (browser, app, refresh tokens) for compromised users. Critical for infostealer response where session cookies may be stolen. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Deletes compromised MFA methods and forces re-registration. Prevents attackers from using stolen authenticator tokens or phone-based MFA. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Adds compromised users to severity-tiered Conditional Access security groups (Blocked/Restricted/Monitored). Sev 25 → block all access, Sev 20 → require compliant device, Sev 5+ → require MFA. Requires: Entra ID P1/P2 + pre-created security groups. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Automatically pushes malicious IP block rules to Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto PAN-OS, or Azure Firewall from SpyCloud infostealer C2 data. Post-deploy: configure firewall API URLs and keys in the Logic App designer. Deploys but remains inactive until firewall credentials are provided.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Isolates infected devices via MDE API. Sev 25 → full isolation (complete disconnect), Sev 20 → selective isolation (allows Outlook/Teams/Defender). Tags device as 'SpyCloud-Compromised' in MDE. Requires: MDE P2. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Emails the exposed user with breach details, required actions (change password, review sign-ins), and support contacts. Uses Office 365 Mail.Send via managed identity. Requires: Exchange Online. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Posts real-time Adaptive Cards to your SOC Teams channel with severity, affected users, exposure type, recommended actions, and direct incident links. Uses webhook (no Graph permissions needed). Post-deploy: add Teams webhook URL in Logic App parameters.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Auto-enriches every Sentinel incident with SpyCloud context: breach source, historical exposure count, password reuse analysis, malware family, and infected device info. Adds tags like 'malware-sourced', 'plaintext-password'. Requires: Sentinel Responder role. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.

RECOMMENDED: ON — Master orchestrator that chains ALL playbooks in 3 phases: Phase 1 (parallel): password reset + session revocation + device isolation → Phase 2 (parallel): MFA enforcement + CA group assignment → Phase 3: SOC notification. Best used as the single automation rule target for severity 25 (session cookie) incidents. Requires: all individual playbook licenses. Post-deploy: auto-granted by post-deploy-auto.sh.
Enable ServiceNow integration for automatic ticket creation when SpyCloud incidents are generated. When enabled, configure the serviceNow* parameters below.
ServiceNow instance URL (e.g., https://your-instance.service-now.com). Required when enableServiceNow is TRUE.
Authentication method for the ServiceNow API connection. Basic = username/password, OAuth = OAuth 2.0 client credentials, ApiKey = ServiceNow API key header.
ServiceNow username for Basic authentication. Must have permissions to create records in the target table. Required when serviceNowAuthType is 'Basic'.
ServiceNow password for Basic authentication. Stored securely and never exposed in deployment outputs or logs. Required when serviceNowAuthType is 'Basic'.

ServiceNow table to create records in. 'incident' = standard IT incidents, 'sn_si_incident' = Security Incident Response (requires SIR plugin), 'change_request' = change records, 'problem' = problem records.
ServiceNow assignment group name for created tickets (e.g., 'SOC Team', 'Security Operations'). Leave blank to use the default assignment group for the target table.
ServiceNow ticket category for created records. 'Security' is recommended for SpyCloud credential exposure incidents.

Template for the ServiceNow short description field. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}, {severity_label}. Example: 'SpyCloud: {severity} exposure detected for {email}'.

JSON string of custom ServiceNow field mappings. Use to set additional fields not covered by the standard parameters. Example: {"u_risk_score": "{severity}", "u_source": "SpyCloud"}. Leave as '{}' if no custom fields are needed.
Enable Jira integration for automatic issue creation when SpyCloud incidents are generated. When enabled, configure the jira* parameters below.
Jira instance URL (e.g., https://your-org.atlassian.net). Required when enableJira is TRUE.
Authentication method for the Jira API connection. ApiToken = email + API token (Jira Cloud), OAuth = OAuth 2.0, PAT = Personal Access Token (Jira Data Center/Server).
Jira account email for ApiToken authentication. Must have permissions to create issues in the target project. Required when jiraAuthType is 'ApiToken'.
Jira API token for authentication. Generate at: https://id.atlassian.net/manage-profile/security/api-tokens. Stored securely and never exposed in deployment outputs or logs.
Jira project key where issues will be created (e.g., 'SEC', 'SOC', 'SECOPS'). The project must already exist in your Jira instance.
Jira issue type for created issues. 'Task' is recommended for SpyCloud credential exposure response work items.
Comma-separated Jira labels applied to created issues. Used for filtering and reporting. Example: 'spycloud,credential-exposure,automated'.

Template for the Jira issue summary field. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {severity_label}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}. Example: '[SpyCloud] {severity_label} credential exposure - {email}'.

JSON string of custom Jira field mappings. Use to set additional fields not covered by standard parameters. Example: {"customfield_10001": "{severity}", "customfield_10002": "SpyCloud"}. Leave as '{}' if no custom fields are needed.
Enable Azure DevOps integration for automatic work item creation when SpyCloud incidents are generated. When enabled, configure the azureDevOps* parameters below.
Azure DevOps organization URL (e.g., https://dev.azure.com/your-org). Required when enableAzureDevOps is TRUE.
Azure DevOps project name where work items will be created. The project must already exist in your Azure DevOps organization. Required when enableAzureDevOps is TRUE.
Azure DevOps work item type for created items. 'Issue' is recommended for SpyCloud credential exposure tracking in Basic process; use 'Task' or 'Bug' for Agile/Scrum processes.
Azure DevOps area path for created work items (e.g., 'ProjectName\Security\Incidents'). Leave blank to use the project root area path.
Azure DevOps iteration path for created work items (e.g., 'ProjectName\Sprint 1'). Leave blank to use the project default iteration.
Azure DevOps user email or display name to assign created work items to. Leave blank to leave unassigned.
Semicolon-separated tags applied to created Azure DevOps work items. Used for filtering and reporting.
Template for the Azure DevOps work item title. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {severity_label}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}. Example: '[SpyCloud] {severity_label} - {email}'.
Enable enhanced Slack notifications for SpyCloud incidents. Sends formatted alert messages to a designated Slack channel via webhook. When enabled, configure the slack* parameters below.
Slack channel for SpyCloud alert notifications (e.g., '#soc-alerts'). The Slack app must be invited to this channel. Required when enableSlackNotifications is TRUE.
Display name for the Slack bot that posts SpyCloud alert notifications.
Slack message format. 'Rich' = Block Kit formatted messages with sections, fields, and action buttons. 'Simple' = plain text messages with minimal formatting.
Filter which incidents are sent to Slack by severity. 'All' = all severities, 'HighCritical' = High and Critical only, 'CriticalOnly' = Critical severity only. Use to reduce notification noise.
Include compromised user details (email, department, manager) in Slack notifications. Set to FALSE if your Slack channel has broad visibility and PII should be minimized.
Enable enhanced Microsoft Teams notifications for SpyCloud incidents. Sends formatted cards to a designated Teams channel. When enabled, configure the teams* parameters below.
Microsoft Teams channel name for SpyCloud alert notifications. The Teams webhook must be configured for this channel. Required when enableTeamsNotifications is TRUE.
Teams card format. 'AdaptiveCard' = modern rich cards with actions and dynamic content (recommended). 'MessageCard' = legacy Office 365 connector card format for backward compatibility.
Filter which incidents are sent to Teams by severity. 'All' = all severities, 'HighCritical' = High and Critical only, 'CriticalOnly' = Critical severity only.
Include actionable remediation buttons in Teams Adaptive Cards (e.g., 'Reset Password', 'Revoke Sessions', 'View in Sentinel'). Only applicable when teamsCardFormat is 'AdaptiveCard'.

Enable enhanced email notifications for SpyCloud incidents. Sends formatted HTML emails with breach details, affected user information, and recommended remediation actions. When enabled, configure the email* parameters below.

Sender email address for SpyCloud alert notifications (e.g., 'sentinel-alerts@company.com'). Must be a valid mailbox in your Office 365 or Exchange Online tenant. Required when enableEmailNotifications is TRUE.
Comma-separated list of recipient email addresses for SpyCloud alert notifications (e.g., 'soc@company.com,security-leads@company.com'). Required when enableEmailNotifications is TRUE.
Comma-separated list of CC email addresses for SpyCloud alert notifications. Leave blank if no CC recipients are needed.
Template for the email subject line. Supports placeholders: {severity}, {severity_label}, {email}, {breach_source}, {exposure_count}. Example: '[SpyCloud Alert] {severity_label} - {email}'.
Include detailed breach source information in email notifications (breach name, date, data types exposed). Provides SOC analysts with full context for triage.
Include recommended remediation actions in email notifications based on the exposure type and severity (e.g., password reset, session revocation, MFA enforcement, device scan).

OPTIONAL: Enable SpyCloud Compass connectors (consumer/partner identity exposure). Requires Enterprise+ API subscription. Set to false if you only have Enterprise tier - Compass connectors will be skipped.

connectorUiConfig.title: SpyCloud Darknet & Identity Threat Exposure Intelligence
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The **SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Connector** for Microsoft Sentinel ingests breach, infostealer, and consumer identity exposure data from SpyCloud's darknet intelligence platform.

---

### Data Tables

| Table | Description | License |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` | Employee credential exposures from breaches and infostealers | Enterprise |
| `SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL` | Breach source metadata (title, category, record counts) | Enterprise |
| `SpyCloudCompassData_CL` | Consumer/partner identity exposures | Enterprise+ |
| `SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL` | Infected device fingerprints and malware artifacts | Enterprise+ |
| `Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL` | MDE device isolation action logs | Enterprise |
| `SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL` | Conditional Access remediation logs | Enterprise |

### Automated Response Playbooks

| Playbook | License | Actions |
|----------|--------|---------|
| **MDE Isolation** | Defender for Endpoint P2 | Isolate compromised devices |
| **Conditional Access** | Entra ID P1+ | Force password reset, revoke sessions |
| **Credential Response** | Entra ID P1+ | Targeted remediation with notifications |
| **MDE Blocklist** | Defender for Endpoint P2 | Block malicious IPs/domains |
| **Firewall Block** | Palo Alto / Fortinet / Azure FW | Push block rules from C2 data |

### Analytics & Hunting

- **38+ Analytics Rules** correlating SpyCloud with Entra ID, MDE, Office 365, DNS, UEBA, Okta, Duo, Ping, firewalls, and Compass
- **15 Hunting Queries** for proactive threat hunting
- **4 Automation Rules** for auto-triage and escalation
- **1 Workbook** with 7 tabbed dashboards and 20+ visualizations
- **4 Watchlists** (VIP, IOC Blocklist, Approved Domains, High-Value Assets)

### Configuration Options

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| Compass Connectors | Off | Enable if you have Enterprise+ subscription |
| UEBA/Entity Analytics | On | Behavioral analytics for exposed users |
| Fusion Detection | Off | Multi-stage attack correlation (enable once per workspace) |
| MSIC Rules | Off | Auto-create incidents from Defender/Entra/MCAS alerts |
| Firewall Rules | On | Palo Alto and Fortinet log correlation |
| IdP Rules | On | Okta, Duo, Ping Identity correlation |

**Requires:** SpyCloud Enterprise API subscription ([portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com))

sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| project TimeGenerated, email, email_domain, target_domain, password_type, severity, infected_machine_id, user_hostname
| take 100

sampleQueries.description: Exposure Count by Corporate Email Domain
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(email_domain)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), UniqueEmails = dcount(email) by email_domain
| sort by ExposureCount desc

SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| project TimeGenerated, email, target_domain, password_type, severity, source_id
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

sampleQueries.description: Infected Devices Grouped by Machine ID and Hostname
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(target_domain), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, user_os
| sort by ExposureCount desc

sampleQueries.description: Watchlist Exposures Enriched with Breach Catalog Metadata
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| join kind=inner (
    SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
    | project source_id=uuid, short_title, breach_category, num_records, confidence
) on source_id
| project TimeGenerated, email, short_title, breach_category, severity, num_records, confidence
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

sampleQueries.description: Recent Breaches from Catalog with Record Counts
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| project TimeGenerated, uuid, short_title, breach_category, num_records, confidence, spycloud_publish_date
| take 50

sampleQueries.description: MDE Remediation Actions — Device Isolation and Tagging Audit
sampleQueries.query:
Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL
| project TimeGenerated, IncidentId, DeviceName, DeviceId, Action, IsolationType, Tag, ActionStatus, Email, Severity
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

sampleQueries.description: Conditional Access Remediation — Password Resets and Session Revocations
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL
| project TimeGenerated, IncidentId, UserPrincipalName, Email, Action, PasswordReset, SessionsRevoked, AddedToCAGroup, ActionStatus, Severity
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

sampleQueries.description: Top 10 Most-Targeted Domains by Exposure Volume
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(target_domain)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), UniqueEmails = dcount(email), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by target_domain
| sort by ExposureCount desc
| take 10

sampleQueries.description: Exposure Timeline — Daily Ingestion Volume by Severity
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize Count = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), severity
| render timechart

sampleQueries.description: Full Health Dashboard — All 6 SpyCloud Tables

union
  (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Watchlist'),
  (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Catalog'),
  (SpyCloudCompassData_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Compass Data'),
  (SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Compass Devices'),
  (Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='MDE Logs'),
  (SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='CA Logs')
| project Table, Records, Latest
| order by Table asc

SpyCloudCompassData_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| summarize Count=count() by severity
| order by severity asc

sampleQueries.description: Compass Infected Devices — Top 25 by Application Count
sampleQueries.query:
SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| top 25 by application_count desc
| project TimeGenerated, user_hostname, user_os, ip_addresses, application_count, infected_time

SpyCloudCompassData_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| join kind=inner (
    SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | project email, w_severity=severity, w_source=source_id
) on email
| summarize CompassHits=count(), WatchlistMatches=dcount(w_source) by email, email_domain
| sort by CompassHits desc
| take 25

instructionSteps.title: Prerequisites & Requirements
instructionSteps.description: Review all requirements before connecting. Incomplete prerequisites will cause deployment failures or limited functionality.

parameters.text:
**Required for All Features:**

1. **SpyCloud Enterprise API Key** — Obtain from [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com). Contact support@spycloud.com if you need Enterprise+ for Compass data.

2. **Azure Roles** (on the resource group):
   - **Microsoft Sentinel Contributor** — Create/manage connectors, analytics rules, workbooks
   - **Log Analytics Contributor** — Create tables, manage data collection rules
   - **Monitoring Metrics Publisher** — Required on the DCR for data ingestion via DCE

3. **Network Access** — Outbound HTTPS (443) to `api.spycloud.io` from Azure. No inbound rules required.

4. **Log Analytics Workspace** — Existing workspace with Microsoft Sentinel enabled, or the template creates one.

---

**Optional License Requirements:**

| Feature | License | What It Enables |
|---------|---------|----------------|
| MDE Isolation Playbook | Defender for Endpoint P2 | Automatic device isolation + security tagging |
| MDE Blocklist Playbook | Defender for Endpoint P2 | Block malicious IPs/domains from infostealer data |
| Conditional Access Playbook | Entra ID P1+ | Force password reset, revoke sessions, CA group assignment |
| Credential Response Playbook | Entra ID P1+ | Targeted UPN-based password reset with notifications |
| UEBA Anomaly Detection | Sentinel UEBA (free) | Behavioral anomaly detection for exposed user accounts |

**What Works Without Extra Licenses:** All data ingestion (5 pollers, 6 tables), 38 analytics rules, workbook dashboards, hunting queries, all notification channels (Slack/Teams/Email/ServiceNow/Jira/Azure DevOps).

---

**Pre-Deployment Checklist:**

- [ ] SpyCloud API key tested: `curl -H 'X-Api-Key: YOUR_KEY' https://api.spycloud.io/enterprise-v2/breach/catalog/1`
- [ ] Required Azure roles assigned to the deploying user
- [ ] Target resource group and Log Analytics workspace identified
- [ ] Decided which playbooks to enable (based on available licenses)
- [ ] Notification channel URLs ready (Teams webhook, Slack webhook, email addresses) if desired

instructionSteps.title: Step 1 — SpyCloud API Key Configuration
instructionSteps.description: Enter your SpyCloud Enterprise Protection API key. This key authenticates all 5 pollers against the SpyCloud API.

parameters.text:
**Where to Find Your API Key:**

1. Log in to [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com)
2. Navigate to **Account Settings** → **API Keys**
3. Copy your **Enterprise API Key** (starts with a long alphanumeric string)

**Important Notes:**
- The API key is stored securely as a `securestring` parameter and is never exposed in logs or outputs
- Enterprise tier is required for Watchlist and Breach Catalog pollers
- Enterprise+ tier is required for Compass Data and Compass Devices pollers
- If you only have Enterprise (not Enterprise+), Compass pollers will return empty results but will not cause errors

**Validation:** After deployment, you can verify API connectivity in the connector health panel or by checking the Data Collection Rule ingestion metrics in Azure Monitor.

parameters.label: SpyCloud API Key
parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.placeholder: Enter your SpyCloud Enterprise API key

instructionSteps.title: Step 2 — Data Filters & Scope
instructionSteps.description: Configure which exposure data to ingest. These settings control the volume, sensitivity, and scope of data flowing into your Sentinel workspace.

parameters.text:
**Understanding SpyCloud Severity Levels:**

| Severity | Category | Description | Typical Volume |
|----------|----------|-------------|---------------|
| **2** | Breach Credential | Username/password exposed in a data breach | High |
| **5** | Breach + PII | Breach data enriched with personally identifiable information | Medium |
| **20** | Infostealer | Credentials stolen by malware (high-risk, active threat) | Medium |
| **25** | Infostealer + Application | Infostealer with specific application targets (highest risk) | Lower |

**Recommendation:** Start with **all severity levels** selected. After initial ingestion, review volume in the workbook and narrow if needed. Severity 20+ (infostealer) records are the highest priority for SOC response.

**Exposure Types:**
- **Corporate** — Exposures matching your organization's email domains
- **Infected** — Exposures from devices infected with infostealer malware (includes session cookies, application credentials)

**Recommendation:** Select both for comprehensive coverage.

parameters.label: Plaintext Passwords
parameters.name: show_plain_password

options.text: Yes — Include plaintext passwords (requires data handling policy)

parameters.placeholder: Include plaintext passwords in ingested data?

parameters.text:
**Plaintext Password Warning:** Enabling plaintext passwords stores cleartext credentials in your Log Analytics workspace. Before enabling:

1. Ensure your organization has a **data handling policy** for exposed credentials
2. Configure **table-level RBAC** on `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` to restrict access
3. Consider your **data retention** policy (passwords should not be retained longer than needed for remediation)
4. The `password_plaintext` column is empty when this setting is 'No'

**Use Case:** Plaintext passwords enable password-reuse detection analytics rules and richer hunting queries.

instructionSteps.title: Step 3 — Polling & Performance Settings
instructionSteps.description: Configure how frequently the connector polls SpyCloud and how it handles retries. These settings affect data freshness, API usage, and resilience.

parameters.text:
**Polling Interval Guidance:**

| Interval | Best For | API Calls/Day | Notes |
|----------|----------|---------------|-------|
| **5 min** | Active incident response | ~1,440 | Highest freshness, highest API usage |
| **15 min** | High-security environments | ~480 | Good balance of freshness and cost |
| **30 min** | Most organizations (recommended) | ~240 | Standard recommendation |
| **60 min** | Large environments, cost-sensitive | ~120 | Acceptable for most SOC workflows |
| **120 min** | Low-priority monitoring | ~60 | May delay detection of active infostealers |
| **360 min** | Minimal monitoring | ~20 | 6-hour gap between checks |

**Lookback Days:** How far back to retrieve data on first connection. 365 days (recommended) ensures you capture the full year of historical exposures. Reduce to 30-90 days if you want faster initial sync.

**Retry & Timeout:** Default values (5 retries, 120s timeout) work for most deployments. Increase timeout to 180-300s if you have very large watchlists (100K+ records).

parameters.label: Initial Lookback Period (days)
parameters.name: lookbackDays
parameters.placeholder: How far back to retrieve on first connection

parameters.label: Retry Count on API Failure
parameters.name: retryCount
parameters.placeholder: Number of retries on transient failures

instructionSteps.title: Step 4 — Architecture & Data Flow
instructionSteps.description: Understand how SpyCloud data flows through your Sentinel environment.

parameters.text:
**Data Pipeline Architecture:**

```
SpyCloud API (api.spycloud.io)
    │
    ▼
CCF Pollers (5 pollers, Codeless Connector Framework)
    │
    ▼
Data Collection Endpoint (DCE) → Data Collection Rule (DCR)
    │                                    │
    │                            Transform & Normalize
    │                                    │
    ▼                                    ▼
Log Analytics Workspace (6 Custom Tables)
    │
    ├── Analytics Rules (38) → Incidents → Playbooks (4)
    ├── Hunting Queries (8) → Bookmarks → Investigations
    ├── Workbook (19 panels) → Dashboards
    └── Copilot Plugin → Natural Language Queries
```

**Table Schema Overview:**

| Table | Key Columns | Records Per Poll |
|-------|------------|------------------|
| `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` | email, severity, source_id, password_type, infected_machine_id, target_domain | Varies by org size |
| `SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL` | uuid, short_title, breach_category, num_records, confidence | ~50-200 total |
| `SpyCloudCompassData_CL` | email, severity, breach_title, infected_machine_id, target_url | Enterprise+ only |
| `SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL` | user_hostname, user_os, ip_addresses, application_count | Enterprise+ only |
| `Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL` | DeviceName, DeviceId, Action, IsolationType, ActionStatus | Playbook output |
| `SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL` | UserPrincipalName, Action, PasswordReset, SessionsRevoked | Playbook output |

**Cost Estimation:** Data ingestion costs depend on your Log Analytics pricing tier and data volume. Most organizations ingest 50MB-500MB/day from SpyCloud. Use the workbook's ingestion volume panel to monitor costs after deployment.

instructionSteps.title: Step 5 — Sentinel Configuration Recommendations
instructionSteps.description: Enable these Microsoft Sentinel features to maximize the value of SpyCloud threat intelligence. Each recommendation includes step-by-step instructions.

parameters.text:
**Critical — Enable These First:**

**1. User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA)**
   - Navigate to: **Sentinel** → **Settings** → **Settings tab** → **User and Entity Behavior Analytics**
   - Toggle: **ON**
   - Select data sources: **Azure Active Directory** (Sign-in Logs, Audit Logs)
   - Why: UEBA creates behavioral baselines for users. When SpyCloud detects an exposed credential, analytics rules correlate with UEBA anomalies to detect active exploitation (e.g., impossible travel after credential exposure).

**2. Fusion Detection**
   - Navigate to: **Sentinel** → **Analytics** → filter by 'Fusion' → **Enable**
   - Why: Fusion uses ML to detect multi-stage attacks. SpyCloud exposure + anomalous sign-in + data exfiltration = high-confidence attack chain.

**3. Threat Intelligence Solution**
   - Navigate to: **Content Hub** → search 'Threat Intelligence' → **Install**
   - Why: Matches SpyCloud infostealer IP addresses against global TI feeds for additional context.

---

**Recommended Sentinel Data Connectors (install from Content Hub):**

| Connector | Priority | Why It Matters |
|-----------|----------|---------------|
| **Entra ID (Azure AD)** | Critical | Sign-in log correlation — detect logins using exposed credentials |
| **Microsoft Defender for Endpoint** | Critical | Device correlation — identify infected machines from SpyCloud data |
| **Office 365** | High | Detect email compromise after credential exposure |
| **Azure Activity** | High | Track admin actions from compromised accounts |
| **DNS** | Medium | Detect C2 callbacks from infostealer-infected devices |
| **Microsoft Cloud App Security** | Medium | Detect OAuth token abuse from stolen session cookies |
| **Okta SSO / Duo / Ping Identity** | Medium | Correlate MFA bypass with exposed credentials |
| **AWS CloudTrail / GCP** | Medium | Cross-cloud compromised account detection |
| **Palo Alto / Fortinet / Zscaler** | Low | Firewall correlation with infostealer C2 traffic |

**Enable Entra ID Connector:**
1. Content Hub → search 'Microsoft Entra ID' → Install
2. Data Connectors → Microsoft Entra ID → Open connector page
3. Enable: Sign-in Logs, Audit Logs, Non-interactive Sign-in Logs, Managed Identity Sign-in Logs
4. These feed 12+ SpyCloud analytics rules that detect sign-ins using exposed credentials.

instructionSteps.title: Step 6 — Connect & Activate
instructionSteps.description: Activate all 5 SpyCloud pollers to begin data ingestion into your Sentinel workspace.

parameters.text:
**Before Clicking Connect:**

1. Verify your API key is entered correctly in Step 1
2. Confirm your severity and exposure type selections in Step 2
3. Review polling settings in Step 3

**What Happens When You Connect:**

1. All 5 pollers activate simultaneously
2. Initial data pull begins using your lookback period (default: 365 days)
3. The first poll may take 5-15 minutes depending on your watchlist size
4. Subsequent polls run at your configured interval
5. Data flows through the DCR transform pipeline and appears in your custom tables

**Expected Initial Ingestion Timeline:**

| Phase | Time | What Happens |
|-------|------|--------------|
| Connection | Immediate | Pollers registered, authentication verified |
| First Data | 5-10 min | Watchlist and Catalog data begins appearing |
| Backfill Complete | 15-60 min | Historical lookback period fully ingested |
| Steady State | Ongoing | Incremental updates at polling interval |
After connecting, proceed to Step 7 for verification queries and troubleshooting guidance.

instructionSteps.title: Step 7 — Verification, Validation & Troubleshooting
instructionSteps.description: Verify data ingestion is working correctly and troubleshoot common issues. Run these queries in your Log Analytics workspace.

parameters.text:
**Verification Queries — Run in Log Analytics:**

**1. Overall Health Check (run first):**
```kql
union
  (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Watchlist'),
  (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Catalog'),
  (SpyCloudCompassData_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Compass Data'),
  (SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | summarize Records=count(), Latest=max(TimeGenerated) | extend Table='Compass Devices')
| project Table, Records, Latest
| order by Table asc
```

**2. Hourly Ingestion Volume:**
```kql
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| render timechart
```

**3. Severity Distribution:**
```kql
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| summarize Count=count() by severity
| order by severity asc
```

**4. Infostealer-Infected Devices:**
```kql
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize ExposureCount=count(), Domains=dcount(target_domain) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname
| order by ExposureCount desc
| take 25
```

**5. Top Exposed Email Domains:**
```kql
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(email_domain)
| summarize Exposures=count(), UniqueEmails=dcount(email) by email_domain
| order by Exposures desc
| take 10
```

**Expected Results:**
- Watchlist table should have records within 10 minutes of connecting
- Catalog table should have 50-200+ breach records
- Compass tables require Enterprise+ (empty with standard Enterprise)
- MDE and CA log tables populate only when playbooks execute

**Troubleshooting Guide:**

| Symptom | Likely Cause | Resolution |
|---------|-------------|------------|
| No data after 15 minutes | API key invalid or wrong tier | Verify key: `curl -H 'X-Api-Key: KEY' https://api.spycloud.io/enterprise-v2/breach/catalog/1` |
| 401 Unauthorized on connect | API key rejected | Ensure Enterprise (not SaaS) tier key from portal.spycloud.com |
| Watchlist data but no Catalog | API permissions | Catalog requires separate endpoint access — contact SpyCloud support |
| Compass tables empty | Enterprise+ required | Compass endpoints need Enterprise+ subscription |
| Playbooks not triggering | Analytics rule disabled | Check Sentinel → Analytics → verify SpyCloud rules are Enabled |
| Playbooks failing (403) | Missing permissions | Run `grant-permissions.sh` from Cloud Shell to assign managed identity roles |
| MDE playbook fails | No MDE P2 license | Device isolation requires Defender for Endpoint Plan 2 |
| CA playbook fails | No Entra P1+ license | Password reset/CA group requires Entra ID P1 or P2 |
| High ingestion costs | Large watchlist | Reduce severity levels, increase polling interval, or reduce retention |
| Duplicate records | Overlapping lookback | Normal during initial sync — deduplication occurs via document_id |

**Advanced Diagnostics:**

1. **Check DCR health:**
   Azure Monitor → Data Collection Rules → `dcr-ccf-{workspace}` → Metrics → check ingestion success/failure rates

2. **Check connector status:**
   Sentinel → Data Connectors → SpyCloud → check 'Last log received' timestamps

3. **Check poller logs:**
   ```kql
   SentinelHealth
   | where OperationName contains 'SpyCloud' or OperationName contains 'DataConnector'
   | where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
   | order by TimeGenerated desc
   ```

4. **Validate DCE endpoint:**
   Azure Monitor → Data Collection Endpoints → `dce-spycloud-{workspace}` → Overview → verify status is 'Succeeded'

**Support Contacts:**
- SpyCloud API issues: support@spycloud.com
- Sentinel connector issues: Open a GitHub issue or contact Microsoft support
- Playbook permissions: Run `grant-permissions.sh` or manually assign roles in Azure AD → Enterprise Applications

instructionSteps.title: Step 8 — Post-Deployment Configuration & Best Practices
instructionSteps.description: Optimize your SpyCloud integration with these post-deployment recommendations for security operations.

parameters.text:
**Immediate Post-Deployment Tasks (Day 1):**

1. **Verify all 6 tables receiving data** — Run the health check query from Step 7
2. **Review analytics rules** — Sentinel → Analytics → filter by 'SpyCloud' → verify all rules are Enabled
3. **Test a playbook manually** — Create a test incident and run the MDE or CA playbook to verify permissions
4. **Configure notification channels** — Update playbook parameters with your Teams/Slack webhook URLs
5. **Review the workbook** — Sentinel → Workbooks → 'SpyCloud Threat Intelligence' → verify panels load

---

**Week 1 Tuning:**

1. **Adjust analytics rule thresholds** — Some rules may generate too many or too few incidents based on your exposure volume
2. **Configure VIP watchlist** — Add high-value accounts (executives, admins, service accounts) to the `SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist` for priority alerting
3. **Review incident volume** — If overwhelmed, increase `spycloudSeverityThreshold` to focus on infostealer (20+) exposures first
4. **Enable automation rules** — Configure auto-assignment, auto-close, or auto-escalation for SpyCloud incidents

---

**Ongoing Operations:**

- **Daily:** Review SpyCloud incidents in Sentinel, triage infostealer alerts (severity 20+) first
- **Weekly:** Check the workbook for exposure trends, review top-exposed domains and users
- **Monthly:** Review playbook execution logs, update VIP watchlist, assess credential remediation rates
- **Quarterly:** Review data retention settings, assess API usage, evaluate Compass data value

---

**Security Hardening:**

1. **Table-level RBAC** — Restrict `SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL` access if plaintext passwords are enabled:
   ```kql
   -- Grant read access only to SOC analysts
   -- Azure Portal → Log Analytics → Tables → SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL → Access Control (IAM)
   ```

2. **Data retention** — Set appropriate retention (90 days recommended for active remediation, 365 for compliance):
   - Configured via the `retentionInDays` ARM template parameter

3. **API key rotation** — Rotate your SpyCloud API key periodically:
   - Generate new key in portal.spycloud.com
   - Update the connector configuration
   - Verify data continues flowing

4. **Managed Identity permissions** — Review and minimize permissions assigned to playbook managed identities

---

**Upgrade Path:**

- **Enterprise → Enterprise+:** Enables Compass pollers for consumer/partner exposure data
- **Add MDE P2:** Enables automatic device isolation for infostealer-infected machines
- **Add Entra P1+:** Enables automatic password reset and Conditional Access enforcement
- **Enable Copilot for Security:** Natural language queries across all SpyCloud data using the included plugin

properties.displayName: [variables('_solutionName')]
properties.descriptionHtml:
The SpyCloud Threat Intelligence Connector integrates SpyCloud's comprehensive breach and infostealer exposure data with Microsoft Sentinel, enabling security teams to detect compromised credentials, investigate infected devices, and automate identity protection responses.

Data Tables

  • SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL — Compromised credentials, PII, and infostealer-stolen data from your organization's monitored assets (email domains, IP ranges, executive watchlists)
  • SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL — Breach source metadata for contextual enrichment, correlation, and impact assessment
  • Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL — Audit log of automated Microsoft Defender for Endpoint remediation actions (device isolation, tagging, IOC submission)
  • SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL — Audit log of automated Azure AD / Entra ID actions (forced password resets, session revocations, Conditional Access group assignments, account disablement)

Key Use Cases

  • Compromised Credential Detection — Identify employee and customer credentials exposed in breaches or stolen by infostealer malware
  • Post-Infection Remediation — Automatically isolate infected devices and force credential resets when infostealer activity is detected
  • Darknet Exposure Monitoring — Continuously monitor for new exposures of corporate assets across SpyCloud's 600B+ recaptured data records
  • Account Takeover Prevention — Detect and block credential stuffing attempts using real-time exposure intelligence
  • Third-Party Risk Assessment — Monitor vendor and supply chain credential exposures for risk scoring
  • Compliance & Audit — Maintain evidence of credential exposure detection and automated remediation for regulatory requirements
  • VIP/Executive Protection — Prioritized monitoring of high-value targets for both credential and PII exposure

Requirements: SpyCloud Enterprise or Enterprise+ API subscription. Contact support@spycloud.com or visit portal.spycloud.com.

definition.description: SpyCloud MDE Device Isolation — Automatically isolates infected devices in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint when SpyCloud detects infostealer malware. Extracts host entities from incidents, searches MDE for matching devices, isolates them (Full or Selective), adds a SpyCloud tag, logs the action, and notifies SOC via Slack, Teams, and Email. REQUIRES: Defender for Endpoint P1/P2. Without MDE, deploys but cannot isolate devices.

definition.description: SpyCloud CA Identity Protection — Automatically resets passwords and revokes sessions in Entra ID when SpyCloud detects compromised credentials. Looks up users via Microsoft Graph, forces immediate password reset, revokes all active sessions, optionally adds to a Conditional Access security group, logs the action, and notifies SOC. properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infostealer Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects when SpyCloud identifies credentials stolen by infostealer malware (severity 20+). These are active infections where malware on a device has exfiltrated passwords, cookies, and session tokens. Requires immediate device investigation and credential reset. Creates an incident with the exposed email as an account entity.
properties.query:
[concat('SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= ', string(parameters('spycloudSeverityThreshold')), '
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize
    ExposureCount = count(),
    Emails = make_set(email, 10),
    TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10),
    MaxSeverity = max(severity),
    LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated)
  by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend
    HostCustomEntity = user_hostname,
    AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])')]

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
properties.description: Fires when no SpyCloud data has been received for more than 2 hours, indicating a potential connector failure or API key issue.

allOf.query: union isfuzzy=true (datatable(LastRecord:datetime)[]) , (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | summarize LastRecord = max(TimeGenerated) | where LastRecord < ago(3h) or isnull(LastRecord))

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infostealer Credential Exposure Detected
properties.description: Detects new infostealer-sourced credential exposures (severity >= 20) from SpyCloud. These indicate a device was actively infected with malware that stole credentials, cookies, and session tokens. Severity 25 includes stolen application data enabling MFA bypass.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(infected_machine_id)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), MaxSeverity = max(severity) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, source_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Plaintext Password Exposure (Critical Risk)
properties.description: Alerts when SpyCloud finds credentials with plaintext (unencrypted) passwords. These credentials can be used immediately by attackers without any cracking. Highest priority for password reset. Check if the password is reused across other services.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by email, email_domain
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — SSN / Financial / Health Data Exposure (Compliance Alert)
properties.description: Detects exposure records containing Social Security numbers, bank account numbers, tax IDs, or health insurance IDs. May trigger breach notification obligations under HIPAA, PCI DSS, GDPR, or state privacy laws.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(social_security_number) or isnotempty(ssn_last_four) or isnotempty(bank_number) or isnotempty(taxid) or isnotempty(health_insurance_id)
| summarize PIITypes = make_set(case(isnotempty(social_security_number), 'SSN-Full', isnotempty(ssn_last_four), 'SSN-Last4', isnotempty(bank_number), 'BankAccount', isnotempty(taxid), 'TaxID', isnotempty(health_insurance_id), 'HealthInsurance', 'Other'), 10), ExposureCount = count() by email, full_name
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Stolen Session Cookies / Tokens (MFA Bypass Risk)
properties.description: Detects when stolen browser cookies or session tokens are found in SpyCloud data. Attackers can use these to bypass MFA entirely by replaying the authenticated session. Requires immediate session revocation and device investigation. This is the most dangerous exposure type.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity == 25
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 15), Devices = make_set(infected_machine_id, 5) by email, user_hostname
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email, HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Device Re-Infection Detected
properties.description: Detects when the same infected_machine_id appears in new SpyCloud exposure records after a previous remediation was logged in the MDE table. Indicates the device was re-compromised after initial remediation, suggesting incomplete cleanup or persistent malware.
properties.query:
let remediated_devices = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | where IsolationStatus == true | distinct InfectedMachineId;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where infected_machine_id in (remediated_devices)
| summarize NewExposures = count(), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, email
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname, AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — User Credentials Exposed Across Multiple Domains
properties.description: Detects when a single user has credentials stolen for 5 or more different target domains, indicating widespread credential reuse or a heavily infected device. High risk of account takeover across multiple services.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize TargetDomainCount = dcount(target_domain), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20), MaxSeverity = max(severity), HasPlaintext = countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)) by email
| where TargetDomainCount >= 5
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Infection from Unusual Geographic Location
properties.description: Detects infostealer infections from country codes that are uncommon in your environment (fewer than 3 previous infections from that country). May indicate targeted attacks, compromised travel devices, or unauthorized access from unexpected locations.
properties.query:
let country_baseline = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | summarize DeviceCount = dcount(infected_machine_id) by country_code | where DeviceCount >= 3 | distinct country_code;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(country_code)
| where country_code !in (country_baseline)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10) by country_code, user_hostname, infected_machine_id
| extend HostCustomEntity = user_hostname

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Credential Observed in Multiple Breach Sources
properties.description: Detects credentials with a sighting count of 3 or more, meaning the same email/password combination has been observed across multiple independent breach sources. This dramatically increases the probability of active credential stuffing attacks using this pair.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where sighting >= 3
| summarize MaxSighting = max(sighting), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10), PasswordTypes = make_set(password_type, 5), LatestExposure = max(TimeGenerated) by email
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — High-Severity Exposure Without Automated Remediation
properties.description: Detects severity 20+ infostealer exposures that have been in the watchlist for over 2 hours but have NO corresponding entry in the Conditional Access remediation logs. This means the automated password reset playbook did not trigger, leaving the compromised credential active.
properties.query:
let remediated_users = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | distinct UserEmail;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(2h)
| where email !in (remediated_users)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), MaxSeverity = max(severity), TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 10) by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Antivirus Present But Failed to Prevent Infostealer
properties.description: Detects infostealer infections on devices that had antivirus software installed, indicating the AV product failed to prevent the malware. Useful for evaluating endpoint protection effectiveness and identifying products that need updated signatures or configuration changes.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where isnotempty(av_softwares)
| mv-expand av_softwares
| summarize InfectedDevices = dcount(infected_machine_id), AffectedUsers = dcount(email) by tostring(av_softwares)
| where InfectedDevices >= 2

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — New Malware Family Appeared in Breach Catalog
properties.description: Fires when a new breach catalog entry appears with a status of 'pending' (newly ingested). This means SpyCloud has identified a new breach or malware source that may contain your organization's data. Early awareness enables proactive response before full ingestion completes.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL
| where status == 'pending'
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| project TimeGenerated, source_id, breach_title, description, status

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Data Ingestion Health Alert
properties.description: Fires when no new SpyCloud watchlist data has been received for more than 3 hours, indicating a potential connector failure, API key expiration, or rate limiting issue. Check SentinelHealth table and the SpyCloud connector page for errors.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| summarize LastRecord = max(TimeGenerated)
| where LastRecord < ago(3h) or isnull(LastRecord)
| extend AlertMessage = strcat('Last SpyCloud data received: ', tostring(LastRecord))

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Okta — Compromised Credential Used in Okta Sign-In
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed email addresses with Okta sign-in events in Sentinel. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials (from SpyCloud) successfully authenticates through Okta, indicating the stolen credentials may be actively exploited. Requires Okta logs ingested via the Okta SSO connector in Sentinel.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
Okta_CL
| where eventType_s == 'user.session.start' and outcome_result_s == 'SUCCESS'
| where actor_alternateId_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = actor_alternateId_s, IPCustomEntity = client_ipAddress_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Duo — Compromised Credential in Duo Authentication
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Cisco Duo authentication logs. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials authenticates through Duo. Requires Duo Admin API logs ingested into Sentinel.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
Duo_CL
| where result_s == 'SUCCESS'
| where email_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email_s, IPCustomEntity = access_device_ip_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Ping Identity — Compromised Credential in PingOne Auth
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Ping Identity/PingOne authentication logs. Requires PingOne logs ingested into Sentinel via syslog or API connector.
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
PingFederate_CL
| where status_s == 'success'
| where subject_s in (compromised_users)
| extend AccountCustomEntity = subject_s, IPCustomEntity = ip_s

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Entra ID — Compromised Credential Sign-In Detected
properties.description: Correlates SpyCloud exposed emails with Entra ID (Azure AD) sign-in logs. Fires when a user with known compromised credentials from SpyCloud successfully signs in, especially from unusual locations or with anomalous patterns. Requires Entra ID sign-in logs enabled (enableEntraSignInLogs=true).
properties.query:
let compromised_users = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | distinct email;
SigninLogs
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserPrincipalName in (compromised_users)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), Locations = make_set(Location, 5), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5) by UserPrincipalName, AppDisplayName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = UserPrincipalName

Entities_-_Get_Accounts.description: Extract account entities from incident for credential response investigation.

body.message:
SpyCloud Credential Response Playbook
User: @{items('For_each_account')?['Name']}@@@{items('For_each_account')?['UPNSuffix']}
Recent sign-ins checked: @{length(body('Check_recent_sign_ins')?['value'])} events
Password reset: Forced on next sign-in
Sessions revoked: All active sessions invalidated
Teams alert: Sent to SOC channel

Notify_Slack.description: Send Slack notification via Incoming Webhook. Only fires if slackWebhookUrl is configured (non-empty). Configure at: api.slack.com → Your Apps → Incoming Webhooks → Add to Channel.

sections.text: Credential exposure detected and automatically remediated. Review the incident in Sentinel for full details.

HTTP_Teams.description: Send rich Teams MessageCard with action buttons (View in Sentinel, View User in Entra). Uses Adaptive Card format with facts table showing user, severity, status, and time.

Notify_Teams.description: Send Microsoft Teams notification via Incoming Webhook with SpyCloud-branded MessageCard. Only fires if teamsWebhookUrl is configured. Configure at: Teams → Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook.

body.content:
SpyCloud Credential Response

Credential exposure response completed: sign-in check, password reset, session revocation.

Notify_Email.description: Send email notification via Microsoft Graph sendMail API. Only fires if notificationEmail is configured. Uses the Logic App managed identity for authentication — requires Mail.Send permission.

body.description: Compromised credentials detected and remediated automatically.

Create_ServiceNow_Incident.description: Create a ServiceNow incident via REST API (/api/now/table/incident). Only fires if serviceNowInstance URL is configured. Fields: short_description, description, urgency=2, impact=2, category=Security, subcategory=Credential Exposure.

Create_Jira_Issue.description: Create a Jira issue via Automation Webhook. Only fires if jiraWebhookUrl is configured. Configure at: Jira → Project → Automation → Create Rule → When: Incoming Webhook. Fields: summary, description, issuetype=Bug, priority=High, labels=[spycloud, credential-exposure, security].

fields.System.Title: SpyCloud: Credential exposure - @{items('For_each_account')?['Name']}@@{items('For_each_account')?['UPNSuffix']}
fields.System.Description: Compromised credentials detected for @{items('For_each_account')?['Name']}@@{items('For_each_account')?['UPNSuffix']}. Auto-remediated: password reset, session revocation.

Create_DevOps_WorkItem.description: Create an Azure DevOps work item via Service Hook. Only fires if azureDevOpsWebhookUrl is configured. Configure at: DevOps → Project Settings → Service hooks. Fields: System.Title, System.Description, WorkItemType=Bug, Priority=2, Tags=SpyCloud;Security.

For_each_account.description: Full response chain: Graph lookup → sign-in analysis → password reset → session revocation → incident comment → Slack → Teams → Email → ServiceNow → Jira → Azure DevOps.

definition.description: SpyCloud Credential Response — Full SOC investigation and remediation workflow. Checks recent sign-in activity for compromised users, forces password reset, revokes sessions, then creates tickets in ServiceNow/Jira/Azure DevOps and sends rich notifications to Slack/Teams/Email. Designed as the primary automated response playbook. REQUIRES: Entra ID P1/P2 for password reset. Notifications work without license.

body.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity == 25 | where isnotempty(infected_machine_id) | where TimeGenerated > ago(24h) | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by infected_machine_id | project infected_machine_id, user_hostname, email, target_domain, severity, ip_addresses, source_id, TimeGenerated

Query_severity_25_exposures.description: KQL query against SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL for severity 25 records with infected_machine_id — these are active infostealer infections with stolen cookies, sessions, and autofill data.

body.Comment: SpyCloud: Severity 25 infostealer infection detected. Device auto-isolated by SpyCloud MDE Blocklist playbook.

Notify_Slack.description: Send Slack notification via Incoming Webhook. Only fires if slackWebhookUrl is configured (non-empty). Configure at: api.slack.com → Your Apps → Incoming Webhooks → Add to Channel.

sections.text: Critical severity 25 infected device automatically isolated.

Notify_Teams.description: Send Microsoft Teams notification via Incoming Webhook with SpyCloud-branded MessageCard. Only fires if teamsWebhookUrl is configured. Configure at: Teams → Channel → Connectors → Incoming Webhook.

body.content:
SpyCloud Blocklist — Sev25 Isolation

Critical severity 25 infected device automatically isolated.

Notify_Email.description: Send email notification via Microsoft Graph sendMail API. Only fires if notificationEmail is configured. Uses the Logic App managed identity for authentication — requires Mail.Send permission.

For_each_infection.description: For each critical infection: search MDE by machine ID, isolate the device, tag it, and send Slack/Teams/Email notification.

definition.description: SpyCloud MDE Blocklist (Scheduled) — Runs on a configurable schedule (default every 4 hours) to scan for severity 25 critical infostealer infections. Queries SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL for the highest severity exposures, searches MDE for matching devices, and isolates them with a 'SpyCloud-Sev25-Infostealer' tag. Proactive defense against the most dangerous infections. REQUIRES: Defender for Endpoint P1/P2. Without MDE, deploys but cannot isolate.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Compromised Credential with Recent Entra Sign-In
properties.description: Detects when a user whose credentials were exposed by SpyCloud has successfully signed into Entra ID within the last 24 hours. This indicates the compromised credential may still be active and the account is at immediate risk of takeover.
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where severity >= 20 | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | distinct email;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where ResultType == 0
| where UserPrincipalName in (exposed)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), IPs = make_set(IPAddress, 5), Apps = make_set(AppDisplayName, 5) by UserPrincipalName
| extend AccountCustomEntity = UserPrincipalName

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — New Exposures with Breach Source Context
properties.description: Enriches new SpyCloud watchlist records with breach catalog details (breach title, description) by joining on source_id. Fires when new high-severity exposures arrive from known breach sources, providing SOC analysts with the full context of where the data came from.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where severity >= 20
| join kind=leftouter (SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | project source_id, breach_title, description, status) on source_id
| where isnotempty(breach_title)
| summarize ExposureCount = count(), Emails = make_set(email, 10) by uuid, breach_title, status
| extend AccountCustomEntity = tostring(Emails[0])

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Executive / VIP Account Exposure
properties.description: Detects when VIP/executive accounts appear in SpyCloud data. Uses the SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist (editable in Sentinel → Watchlists) for dynamic matching. Falls back to common executive patterns (ceo, cfo, cto, ciso, admin, root) if watchlist is empty. Edit the watchlist to add your organization's specific executives.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Same Password Reused Across Multiple Target Domains
properties.description: Detects when the same password hash appears in exposures targeting 3 or more different domains for the same user. This indicates dangerous credential reuse — if one account is compromised, all accounts with the same password are at risk.
properties.query:
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where isnotempty(password) or isnotempty(password_plaintext)
| extend pw_hash = hash_sha256(coalesce(password_plaintext, password))
| summarize TargetDomains = make_set(target_domain, 20), DomainCount = dcount(target_domain) by email, pw_hash
| where DomainCount >= 3
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Exposure Older Than 7 Days Without Remediation
properties.description: Detects high-severity (20+) exposures that have been in the watchlist for more than 7 days but have no corresponding entry in either the MDE or CA remediation logs. This is a compliance and SLA alert — these exposures should have been remediated within your organization's defined response window.
properties.query:
let mde_remediated = Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL | distinct InfectedMachineId;
let ca_remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | distinct UserEmail;
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where severity >= 20
| where TimeGenerated < ago(7d)
| where infected_machine_id !in (mde_remediated)
| where email !in (ca_remediated)
| summarize DaysExposed = datetime_diff('day', now(), min(TimeGenerated)), ExposureCount = count() by email, infected_machine_id
| extend AccountCustomEntity = email
