properties.displayName: SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × DNS — Infected User Resolving Malicious Domains
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20
| distinct email, target_domain;
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where Name has_any (exposed | project target_domain)
| join kind=inner exposed on $left.Name == $right.target_domain
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, Name, email, target_domain, QueryType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Network — Infected IP in Device Network Events
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20
| mv-expand ip = ip_addresses
| extend src_ip = tostring(ip)
| where isnotempty(src_ip)
| distinct src_ip, email, infected_machine_id;
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| where RemoteIP in~ (ips | project src_ip)
| join kind=inner ips on $left.RemoteIP == $right.src_ip
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, email, infected_machine_id

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × MDE Devices — Infected Machine in Defender Inventory
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × MDE Devices — Infected Machine in Defender Inventory
properties.query:
let devices = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 and isnotempty(user_hostname)
| distinct user_hostname, email, severity, infected_machine_id;
DeviceInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
| join kind=inner devices on $left.DeviceName == $right.user_hostname
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, DeviceId, OSPlatform, email, severity, infected_machine_id

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × MDE Alerts — Exposure Correlated with Defender Alert
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × MDE Alerts — Exposure Correlated with Defender Alert
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5
| distinct email;
AlertInfo
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| join kind=inner AlertEvidence on AlertId
| where EntityType == 'User'
| extend upn = tolower(strcat(AccountName, '@', AccountDomain))
| where upn in~ (exposed)
| project TimeGenerated, AlertId, Title, Severity, upn, Category

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Cloud Apps — Compromised User Accessing SaaS
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Cloud Apps — Compromised User Accessing SaaS
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5
| distinct email;
CloudAppEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| where AccountId in~ (exposed)
| where ActionType in ('FileDownloaded','FileUploaded','FileShared','MailSend')
| summarize Actions=count(), Apps=dcount(Application) by AccountId, Application, ActionType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Entra Risk — Exposed User Flagged Risky
properties.description: Cross-data correlation rule: SpyCloud × Entra Risk — Exposed User Flagged Risky
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5
| distinct email;
AADUserRiskEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(2d)
| where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, RiskLevel, RiskState, RiskDetail

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Office 365 — Compromised User Mail Forwarding
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Office 365 — Compromised User Mail Forwarding
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email;
OfficeActivity | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where Operation in ('New-InboxRule','Set-InboxRule') and UserId in~ (exposed) | where Parameters has 'ForwardTo' or Parameters has 'RedirectTo' | project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, Parameters, ClientIP

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Email — Phishing Targeting Exposed Users
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Email — Phishing Targeting Exposed Users
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
EmailEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where RecipientEmailAddress in~ (exposed) and ThreatTypes has 'Phish' | project TimeGenerated, SenderFromAddress, RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, ThreatTypes

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Identity — Lateral Movement by Exposed User
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Identity — Lateral Movement by Exposed User
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
IdentityLogonEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where AccountUpn in~ (exposed) and LogonType in ('Interactive','RemoteInteractive') | where DestinationDeviceName != DeviceName | project TimeGenerated, AccountUpn, DeviceName, DestinationDeviceName, LogonType

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × TI — Infection IPs in Threat Intelligence
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × TI — Infection IPs in Threat Intelligence
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | mv-expand ip = ip_addresses | extend src_ip = tostring(ip) | where isnotempty(src_ip) | distinct src_ip;
ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and isnotempty(NetworkIP) | where NetworkIP in (ips) | project TimeGenerated, NetworkIP, ThreatType, ConfidenceScore, Description

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × UEBA — Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × UEBA — Anomalous Behavior from Exposed User
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email;
BehaviorAnalytics | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed) | where ActivityInsights has 'True' or InvestigationPriority > 3 | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, ActivityType, InvestigationPriority

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Azure — Exposed User Modifying Cloud Resources
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Azure — Exposed User Modifying Cloud Resources
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
AzureActivity | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) | where Caller in~ (exposed) | where OperationNameValue has 'write' or OperationNameValue has 'delete' | where ActivityStatusValue == 'Success' | project TimeGenerated, Caller, OperationNameValue, ResourceGroup

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Compass — Consumer + Corporate Cross-Reference
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud Compass — Consumer + Corporate Cross-Reference
properties.query: SpyCloudCompassData_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | join kind=inner (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | distinct email, severity) on email | project TimeGenerated, email, breach_title, target_domain, CompassSev=severity, CorporateSev=severity1

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Firewall — Infected IP in Security Logs
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Firewall — Infected IP in Security Logs
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 20 | mv-expand ip = ip_addresses | extend src_ip = tostring(ip) | where isnotempty(src_ip) | distinct src_ip;
CommonSecurityLog | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d) | where SourceIP in (ips) or DestinationIP in (ips) | project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, DestinationIP, DeviceAction, DeviceVendor

properties.displayName: SpyCloud × Impossible Travel — Sign-In from Unexpected Country
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud × Impossible Travel — Sign-In from Unexpected Country
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email, country_code;
SigninLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) and ResultType == 0 | where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed | project email) | extend cc = tostring(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion) | join kind=inner (exposed | project email, sc_cc=country_code) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.email | where cc != sc_cc and isnotempty(cc) | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, cc, sc_cc, IPAddress

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Compass — Device Reinfection Detected
properties.description: Cross-data: SpyCloud Compass — Device Reinfection Detected
properties.query: SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | join kind=inner (SpyCloudCompassDevices_CL | where TimeGenerated between(ago(60d) .. ago(7d)) | distinct user_hostname, user_os) on user_hostname | project TimeGenerated, user_hostname, user_os, application_count, ip_addresses

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Entra ID Protection alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents when Entra ID Protection detects risky users or risky sign-ins. Correlate with SpyCloud data to determine if the risky user was flagged due to darknet credential exposure.

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Defender for Cloud alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from Microsoft Defender for Cloud (Azure Defender) alerts covering VMs, SQL, Storage, Key Vault, App Service, Kubernetes, and more. Cross-correlate with SpyCloud to detect compromised credentials accessing cloud resources.

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Microsoft Cloud App Security alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (MCAS) alerts. Detects impossible travel, suspicious inbox rules, OAuth app abuse, and mass file downloads — correlate with SpyCloud to verify if compromised credentials were used.

properties.displayName: Create incidents from Defender for Identity alerts
properties.description: Auto-creates Sentinel incidents from Microsoft Defender for Identity alerts (on-premises Active Directory). Detects pass-the-hash, golden ticket, DCSync, and reconnaissance activities — critical to correlate with SpyCloud credential exposures that may enable these attacks.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Enrich and Tag Credential Exposure Incidents
properties.description: Automatically sets severity and adds investigation tasks for SpyCloud credential exposure incidents. To auto-trigger playbooks, configure the RunPlaybook action manually after granting Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor role to the playbook's managed identity.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud VIP/Executive Watchlist
properties.description: Editable list of VIP and executive email addresses for elevated SpyCloud alerting. Add emails to this watchlist and Rule #20 (Executive/VIP Exposure) will dynamically match against it. Edit in Sentinel → Watchlists → SpyCloud-VIP-Watchlist.
properties.rawContent:
Email,Name,Title,Priority
ceo@company.com,CEO,Chief Executive Officer,Critical
cfo@company.com,CFO,Chief Financial Officer,Critical
cto@company.com,CTO,Chief Technology Officer,Critical
ciso@company.com,CISO,Chief Information Security Officer,Critical
admin@company.com,IT Admin,Domain Administrator,High

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Exposed Users with Active Sign-Ins
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email, severity, breach_title;
SigninLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(2d) and ResultType == 0 | where UserPrincipalName in~ (exposed | project email) | join kind=inner exposed on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.email | summarize SignIns=count(), Apps=dcount(AppDisplayName), IPs=dcount(IPAddress) by UserPrincipalName, severity, breach_title | order by severity desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Finds users whose credentials were exposed in SpyCloud AND who have successful Entra ID sign-ins in the last 48 hours. These are the highest-priority accounts — an attacker may already be using the stolen credentials.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Infected IPs Across All Network Logs
properties.query:
let ips = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | mv-expand ip = ip_addresses | extend src_ip = tostring(ip) | where isnotempty(src_ip) | distinct src_ip;
union isfuzzy=true
(DeviceNetworkEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where RemoteIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by RemoteIP, DeviceName | extend Source="MDE"),
(DnsEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where ClientIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by ClientIP | extend Source="DNS"),
(CommonSecurityLog | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where SourceIP in (ips) or DestinationIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by SourceIP, DestinationIP | extend Source="Firewall"),
(ThreatIntelligenceIndicator | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | where NetworkIP in (ips) | summarize Hits=count() by NetworkIP, ThreatType | extend Source="TI")
| order by Hits desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Sweeps MDE network events, DNS, firewall (CEF), and Threat Intelligence feeds for any IP addresses associated with SpyCloud infostealer infections. Comprehensive network-level threat hunt.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Password Reuse Across Domains
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) and isnotempty(password) | summarize Domains=dcount(target_domain), DomainList=make_set(target_domain, 10), Exposures=count(), MaxSev=max(severity) by email, password | where Domains >= 3 | project email, Domains, DomainList, Exposures, MaxSev | order by Domains desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies users whose same password hash appears across 3+ different target domains — indicating dangerous password reuse. These users are vulnerable to credential stuffing attacks across all their accounts.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Devices Infected Multiple Times
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) and isnotempty(infected_machine_id) | summarize Infections=count(), Sources=dcount(source_id), Users=dcount(email), FirstSeen=min(spycloud_publish_date), LastSeen=max(spycloud_publish_date) by infected_machine_id, user_hostname, user_os | where Infections > 1 | order by Infections desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Finds devices that appear in SpyCloud data across multiple breach sources or time periods — indicating the device was not properly remediated after the first infection, or the user continues risky behavior.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Plaintext Passwords by Domain
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) and isnotempty(password_plaintext) | summarize PlaintextCount=count(), Users=dcount(email), Domains=dcount(target_domain), MaxSev=max(severity) by email_domain | order by PlaintextCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Aggregates all plaintext (cleartext) password exposures by email domain. These credentials can be used immediately by attackers without any cracking. Priority for password reset and user notification.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Compass Consumer + Corporate Overlap
properties.query: SpyCloudCompassData_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) and severity >= 20 | join kind=inner (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | distinct email, severity) on email | summarize CompassHits=count(), CorporateMaxSev=max(severity1), CompassMaxSev=max(severity), Domains=dcount(target_domain) by email | order by CorporateMaxSev desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Cross-references SpyCloud Compass (consumer identity) data with corporate watchlist data. Users appearing in both datasets are reusing personal credentials for corporate accounts — a major risk vector.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Unremediated High-Severity Exposures
properties.query:
let remediated = SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) | distinct tolower(Email);
SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | where tolower(email) !in (remediated) | summarize Exposures=count(), MaxSev=max(severity), Domains=dcount(target_domain), LatestExposure=max(TimeGenerated) by email | order by MaxSev desc, Exposures desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies high-severity (20+) exposures that have NOT been remediated by the CA playbook. These are the most urgent action items — exposed credentials without a password reset.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Risk Score Dashboard
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d) | summarize Exposures=count(), MaxSev=max(severity), PlaintextPWs=countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)), Devices=dcountif(infected_machine_id, isnotempty(infected_machine_id)), Domains=dcount(target_domain), DaysSince=datetime_diff('day',now(),max(TimeGenerated)) by email | extend Score = Exposures*2 + MaxSev*3 + PlaintextPWs*10 + Devices*15 + iff(DaysSince<7,20,iff(DaysSince<30,10,0)) | extend Risk=case(Score>=100,"CRITICAL",Score>=50,"HIGH",Score>=20,"MEDIUM","LOW") | order by Score desc | take 100

tags.name: description
tags.value: Calculates a composite risk score for every exposed user based on severity, plaintext passwords, device infections, target domain count, and recency. Use to prioritize remediation efforts.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Escalate Critical Severity Incidents
properties.description: Automatically sets severity to High and assigns owner tag for SpyCloud incidents involving severity 25 (critical infostealer) or plaintext password exposure. Ensures immediate SOC attention.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Task Device Infection Incidents
properties.description: Automatically adds investigation tasks for incidents involving infected devices or device reinfection patterns detected by SpyCloud Compass.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud — Auto-Close Low-Risk Informational Alerts
properties.description: Automatically closes informational-severity incidents (e.g., new malware family in catalog, AV-present-but-failed) after tagging them for tracking. Reduces SOC noise while preserving audit trail.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud IOC Blocklist
properties.description: Indicators of Compromise collected from SpyCloud infostealer data — malicious IPs, domains, and URLs associated with credential theft infrastructure. Used by hunting queries and analytics rules for automated blocking. Add entries manually or via the MDE Blocklist playbook.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Approved/Corporate Domains
properties.description: List of approved corporate email domains for SpyCloud monitoring scope. Analytics rules reference this watchlist to focus alerting on corporate accounts vs. personal accounts. Add all your organization's email domains here.
properties.rawContent:
Domain,Type,Priority,Notes

company.io,Secondary,High,Engineering domain

properties.displayName: SpyCloud High-Value Assets
properties.description: Critical servers, databases, and applications that require elevated monitoring when accessed by users with SpyCloud credential exposures. Used by hunting queries to prioritize investigation of compromised users accessing sensitive systems.
properties.rawContent:
AssetName,AssetType,Criticality,Owner,Notes
DC01,DomainController,Critical,IT-Infrastructure,Primary domain controller

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Stolen Session Cookies Used for MFA Bypass
properties.query:
let stolen_sessions = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | where isnotempty(infected_machine_id) and (isnotempty(cookie_domain) or log_id contains "cookie" or log_id contains "session") | distinct email, infected_machine_id, target_domain, severity;
SigninLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) and ResultType == 0 | where AuthenticationRequirement == "singleFactorAuthentication" or TokenIssuerType == "AzureAD" | where UserPrincipalName in~ (stolen_sessions | project email) | join kind=inner stolen_sessions on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.email | summarize SessionReuses=count(), UniqueIPs=dcount(IPAddress), Countries=dcount(LocationDetails.countryOrRegion) by UserPrincipalName, severity, target_domain | where UniqueIPs > 1 | order by SessionReuses desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies users with stolen session cookies (from SpyCloud infostealer data) who have recent sign-ins bypassing MFA. Multiple unique IPs suggest token replay attacks.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Lateral Movement from Compromised Accounts
properties.query:
let compromised = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
IdentityLogonEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where AccountUpn in~ (compromised) | where LogonType in ("RemoteInteractive", "Network", "CachedRemoteInteractive") | summarize UniqueTargets=dcount(DestinationDeviceName), Logons=count(), LogonTypes=make_set(LogonType) by AccountUpn, SourceDeviceName = DeviceName | where UniqueTargets > 2 | order by UniqueTargets desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Hunts for lateral movement by compromised users — accounts with SpyCloud exposure that authenticated to 3+ different machines via RDP, SMB, or remote logons. Indicates active use of stolen credentials.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Data Exfiltration After Credential Exposure
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email, spycloud_publish_date;
CloudAppEvents | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where ActionType in ("FileDownloaded", "FileSyncDownloadedFull", "FileModifiedExtended", "FileCopied") | where AccountObjectId != "" | join kind=inner (IdentityInfo | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) | distinct AccountUPN, AccountObjectId) on AccountObjectId | where AccountUPN in~ (exposed | project email) | summarize Downloads=count(), UniqueFiles=dcount(ObjectName), DataVolumeMB=sum(toint(RawEventData.FileSizeBytes))/1048576 by AccountUPN, ActionType | where Downloads > 50 | order by Downloads desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Detects mass file downloads or data exfiltration from cloud apps by users whose credentials were exposed in SpyCloud. Tracks download volume and unique files to identify data theft post-compromise.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Mailbox Compromise and Forwarding Rules
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 5 | distinct email;
OfficeActivity | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where Operation in ("New-InboxRule", "Set-InboxRule", "Set-Mailbox", "Set-TransportRule") | where UserId in~ (exposed) | extend RuleDetails = tostring(Parameters) | where RuleDetails contains "Forward" or RuleDetails contains "Redirect" or RuleDetails contains "Delete" | project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, RuleDetails, ClientIP | order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Searches for mailbox forwarding rules, redirect rules, or deletion rules created by users with SpyCloud credential exposure. Common BEC and account takeover technique — attacker sets auto-forward to external mailbox.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Privilege Escalation by Compromised Users
properties.query:
let exposed = SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) and severity >= 20 | distinct email;
AuditLogs | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) | where OperationName in ("Add member to role", "Add eligible member to role", "Add owner to group", "Add member to group", "Consent to application", "Add app role assignment to service principal") | where InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName in~ (exposed) | project TimeGenerated, InitiatedBy=tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName), Operation=OperationName, Target=tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName), Role=tostring(TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties[0].newValue) | order by TimeGenerated desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Detects privilege escalation activities by users whose credentials were exposed in SpyCloud — role assignments, group additions, app consents, and service principal modifications. May indicate an attacker using stolen credentials to elevate access.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Malware Family Trends and Campaign Tracking
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) and severity >= 20 | where isnotempty(infected_path) or isnotempty(infected_machine_id) | extend malware_family = case(infected_path contains "redline", "RedLine", infected_path contains "raccoon", "Raccoon", infected_path contains "vidar", "Vidar", infected_path contains "lumma", "LummaC2", infected_path contains "stealc", "StealC", infected_path contains "aurora", "Aurora", infected_path contains "meta", "MetaStealer", infected_path contains "titan", "TitanStealer", "Unknown") | summarize Victims=dcount(email), Devices=dcount(infected_machine_id), FirstSeen=min(spycloud_publish_date), LastSeen=max(spycloud_publish_date), Domains=dcount(target_domain) by malware_family | order by Victims desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Tracks infostealer malware family trends from SpyCloud data — which malware families are targeting your organization, victim counts, device infections, and campaign timelines. Essential for threat intelligence reporting.

properties.displayName: SpyCloud Hunt — Third-Party Breach Impact Assessment
properties.query: SpyCloudBreachCatalog_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) | join kind=inner (SpyCloudBreachWatchlist_CL | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d) | summarize AffectedUsers=dcount(email), MaxSev=max(severity), PlaintextPWs=countif(isnotempty(password_plaintext)), HasCookies=countif(isnotempty(infected_machine_id)) by source_id) on source_id | project BreachTitle=title, BreachDate=breach_date, AffectedUsers, MaxSev, PlaintextPWs, HasCookies, Description=description | order by AffectedUsers desc

tags.name: description
tags.value: Assesses the organizational impact of each third-party breach or infostealer campaign in the SpyCloud catalog. Shows which breaches affect the most users, have plaintext passwords, or involve device infections.

value.step1_mde_permissions: REQUIRED (if MDE playbook enabled): Assign Machine.Isolate + Machine.ReadWrite.All to the MDE Logic App managed identity via PowerShell
value.step2_graph_permissions: REQUIRED (if CA playbook enabled): Assign User.ReadWrite.All + Directory.ReadWrite.All to the CA Logic App managed identity via PowerShell
value.step3_sentinel_automation: REQUIRED (if automation rule enabled): Assign 'Microsoft Sentinel Automation Contributor' to the Sentinel service principal
value.step4_monitoring_publisher: REQUIRED (if playbooks enabled): Assign 'Monitoring Metrics Publisher' on the DCR to both Logic App identities
value.step5_key_vault_access: OPTIONAL: If using Key Vault with Logic Apps, assign Key Vault Secrets User role to Logic App identities
value.step6_defender_xdr: OPTIONAL: Enable Microsoft Defender XDR connector via Sentinel → Content Hub
value.step7_copilot_plugin: OPTIONAL: Upload SpyCloud_Copilot_Plugin_Ultimate.yaml to Security Copilot → Sources → Custom
value.step8_copilot_agent: OPTIONAL: Upload SpyCloud_Agent_Manifest.yaml via Security Copilot → Build → Upload YAML manifest
value.step0_dcr_dce: REQUIRED (if playbooks enabled): Get the DCE Logs Ingestion URI and DCR Immutable ID. Go to Azure Portal → Monitor → Data Collection Endpoints → copy Logs Ingestion URI. Then Monitor → Data Collection Rules → JSON View → copy immutableId. Update the Logic App parameters with these values, or redeploy the template with them filled in.

value.note: Run scripts/post-deploy.sh to auto-resolve DCE URI and DCR Immutable ID
ALL rules are deployed DISABLED. Review each rule's KQL logic, then enable individually in Sentinel → Analytics.
value.idpNote: Identity Provider correlation rules (Okta, Duo, Ping, Entra) require those IdP logs to be ingested into Sentinel first via their respective Content Hub connectors.

value.okta: To ingest Okta logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'Okta' → Install → Configure with Okta API token. Table: Okta_CL
value.duo: To ingest Duo logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'Cisco Duo' → Install → Configure with Duo Admin API credentials. Table: Duo_CL
value.ping: To ingest Ping Identity logs: Configure syslog forwarding from PingFederate to Sentinel via AMA agent, or use the PingOne API connector. Table: PingFederate_CL
value.cyberark: To ingest CyberArk logs: Sentinel → Content Hub → search 'CyberArk' → Install → Configure CEF/syslog forwarding. Table: CyberArk_CL
value.entraId: Entra ID sign-in/audit logs: Enable via this template's enableEntraSignInLogs and enableEntraAuditLogs parameters, or manually via Entra ID → Diagnostic Settings.
value.defenderXdr: Defender XDR: Enable via Sentinel → Content Hub → Microsoft Defender XDR → Install & Connect. Provides AlertInfo, DeviceEvents, IdentityLogonEvents tables.
value.exchangeOnline: Exchange/O365: Enable via Sentinel → Content Hub → Microsoft 365 → Install. Provides OfficeActivity, EmailEvents tables for phishing correlation.

value.note: Entra ID diagnostic settings CANNOT be deployed via ARM templates. Configure manually:
value.signInLogs: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check SignInLogs, NonInteractiveUserSignInLogs → Send to Log Analytics workspace → select your workspace
value.auditLogs: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check AuditLogs → Send to Log Analytics workspace → select your workspace
value.riskySignIns: Entra ID → Monitoring → Diagnostic settings → Add diagnostic setting → check RiskyUsers, UserRiskEvents → Send to Log Analytics workspace

value.note: Run scripts/post-deploy.sh after deployment to resolve DCE/DCR and assign RBAC + API permissions.
